$description: American Industrial — canonical design tokens in DTCG format. See design-tokens.css for CSS custom property version.

$meta.aesthetic: Structural-honesty industrial design: condensed heavy display type, shared-border grids, flat accent rules, no decoration — built for authority.

color.$description: American Industrial color system. Foundation: high-contrast neutrals. Accent: a single project-chosen color applied structurally (bars, rules, active states). Never decorative.

palette.$description: Signature accent colors. One per project. Restraint is the rule.

rust.$description: Primary accent — accent bars, active states, CTAs, feature left borders

brass.$description: Secondary accent — data numerals, secondary CTAs

steel.$description: Structural gray — grid borders, divider lines

signal-orange.$description: Optional accent rotation — high-energy projects (VEKTOR, Brass Hands)

cold-technical.$description: Optional accent rotation — tech/defense projects (ARC Division)

warm-neutral.$description: Optional accent rotation — understated projects (Design Ops Unit)

soft-signal.$description: Optional accent rotation — warm signal projects (Atlas)

neutral.$description: The structural neutral scale. High-contrast foundation — always present regardless of accent choice.

black.$description: Pure black — darkest background or primary text

off-black.$description: Dark sections, navigation — slightly lighter than black

dark.$description: Nav, footer, dark sections + body text — warm-tinted dark

bg.$description: Main light background — warm cream paper

cream.$description: Text on dark backgrounds — warm off-white

white.$description: Pure white — text on accent-colored backgrounds

dark-mode.$description: Command Center palette — dark mode equivalent. Same rules: no gradients, no glows, square corners.

bg.$description: Deep navy — dark mode main background (not pure black)

accent.$description: {company} accent — replaces rust in dark mode only

divider.$description: Divider line color — warm-tinted at low opacity

semantic.$description: Semantic role tokens — map palette tokens to functional roles. Components reference these, never raw palette tokens.

text-on-dark.$description: Text on dark or accent-colored backgrounds

accent.$description: Primary accent — used structurally (bars, active states, CTAs). Override with accent rotation class.

accent-secondary.$description: Secondary accent — data highlights, secondary CTAs

display.$description: Display/headline face — condensed, heavy. Used for ALL headlines, labels, nav. Always uppercase.

body.$description: Body face — readable at 16px, generous line-height. Never use as display face.

mono.$description: Monospace face — data, timestamps, status labels ONLY. Never for section labels (use display instead).

light.$description: Labels, secondary elements — light weight display type

bold.$description: Headlines, display type — minimum weight for display use

fontSize.$description: Fluid type scale using clamp(min, preferred, max). Industrial type creates extreme contrast between hero and body.

hero.$description: Hero headline — display font, uppercase, lh 0.85–0.95

h1.$description: Section title — display font, uppercase, lh 1.05

label.$description: Labels, nav — display font, uppercase, 0.12–0.3em tracking

caption.$description: Footnotes, data labels — display or mono, uppercase

letterSpacing.$description: Tracking tiers. Industrial type has tight headlines and highly expanded labels — the contrast is intentional.

headlines.$description: Display headlines — slight open tracking at scale

label.$description: UI labels — expanded, always paired with uppercase

expanded.$description: Section heads, caps labels — maximum expansion

body.$description: Body text — never add tracking to body text

display.$description: Hero/display — compressed, lines nearly touch. Industrial weight requires tight setting.

heading.$description: Section titles — still tight, creates visual mass

body.$description: Body text — generous for readability; contrast with tight headlines creates rhythm

space.$description: Spacing scale. Industrial design uses generous section padding and tight component spacing.

radius.$description: Border-radius scale. The defining rule: all interactive elements use square corners. Non-zero radius only for non-interactive elements.

none.$description: THE RULE — all interactive elements (buttons, inputs, cards)

border.$description: Named border styles. Borders are the structural backbone — not shadows.

accent.$description: Accent bars, active states — the signature structural marker

motion.$description: Easing and timing. Industrial motion is smooth and purposeful — never bouncy or playful.

ease-cinematic.$description: Card hover, text reveal — dramatic deceleration

forbidden.$description: Anti-patterns. The audit skill greps these as a blocklist.
